assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can run local tooling required by the selected 5 Minute Intro route.
No FusionAuth instance, administrator, application, or sample Express app exists yet.
The first administrator account is created in the FusionAuth Setup Wizard rather than on a hosted SaaS signup form for the self-hosted routes.

developer_goal: Stand up FusionAuth through the 5 Minute Intro and complete login to the sample Express application so the home page greets the authenticated administrator by first name.

first_success.outcome: The registered administrator logs into the sample Express application through FusionAuth and returns to the home page with a personalized greeting.
first_success.observable_signal: After login, the browser returns to the application home page and displays the user's first name; the guide states authentication data is stored in the Express session.

route_selection.rule: Choose among Docker, FastPath, and Sandbox only after lexicographic comparison of those documented peers to the same login terminal; do not invent a winner when environment-conditioned recommendations conflict with fewest-actions ranking.

candidates.name: 5 Minute Intro Docker route
candidates.reason: Documented as the best choice when Docker and Docker Compose are installed, with a complete Setup Wizard through sample-app login path, but Sandbox is documented as the quickest no-install test drive to the same functioning login outcome.

candidates.name: 5 Minute Intro FastPath route
candidates.reason: Peer route when a local PostgreSQL or MySQL database is already installed; different install actions before the shared application configuration.

candidates.name: 5 Minute Intro hosted Sandbox route
candidates.reason: Documented as the quickest way to test drive without installing anything, reaching the same functioning application login outcome, while warning the environment is public, shared, and regularly wiped.

required_path.action: Install and start FusionAuth using the selected 5 Minute Intro installation route.

required_fields.label: Route-specific install inputs
required_fields.notes: Docker requires compose files and .env password edits; FastPath and Sandbox change or remove those inputs.

required_path.observable_result: FusionAuth is reachable for Setup Wizard or Sandbox administration.

required_path.action: Complete the Setup Wizard by creating the administrator account and accepting the license.
required_path.observable_result: The developer is logged into the FusionAuth admin UI as the new administrator.

required_path.action: Create an Application, configure OAuth redirect and logout URLs, and copy Client Id and Client Secret.
required_path.observable_result: OAuth credentials exist for the sample application.

required_path.action: Register the administrator to the Application and ensure a first name exists for the greeting.

required_fields.label: First name
required_fields.notes: Required if missing so the sample greeting can display it.

required_path.observable_result: The administrator can authorize against the sample Application.

required_path.action: Clone the sample guide app, configure CLIENT_ID and CLIENT_SECRET, install dependencies, and start the Express app.
required_path.observable_result: The sample app listens on localhost:3000.

required_path.action: Open the sample app, choose Login, and authenticate with the Setup Wizard administrator.
required_path.observable_result: The home page greets the user by first name after successful authentication.

prerequisites.description: Node.js is required for the sample Express application on the Docker 5 Minute route.
Docker and Docker Compose are required if the Docker install peer is selected.

external_gates.description: The Setup Wizard requires license acceptance before the administrator session continues.

unavoidable_waits.description: Docker Compose must pull and start FusionAuth and dependent containers before the Setup Wizard is available.

platform_outcomes.description: FusionAuth creates the administrator account and logs the developer into the admin UI after Setup Wizard submit.
Successful sample-app login stores user data in the Express session and shows the personalized greeting.

excluded.item: Reading Get Started and 5 Minute Intro docs as measured actions
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is evidence, not a product action.

excluded.item: Self-service registration, themes, and other Next Steps
excluded.reason: The guide places these after the functioning login terminal.

excluded.item: Framework-specific QuickStarts beyond the 5 Minute Intro sample
excluded.reason: Get Started points to the 5 Minute Intro as the general interactive introduction.

sources.title: Get Started
evidence_supported: Entry to FusionAuth getting started and 5 Minute Intro

sources.title: 5 Minute Intro
evidence_supported: Docker, FastPath, and Sandbox peer choices
Shared functioning login and logout outcome

sources.title: Sandbox
evidence_supported: Hosted Sandbox as no-install peer route

uncertainties.question: Which 5 Minute Intro peer is the selected shortest path: Docker, FastPath, or Sandbox?
uncertainties.impact: Sandbox minimizes install actions while Docker is recommended when Docker is present; the peers change prerequisites, waits, and whether Setup Wizard runs on localhost or a shared public host.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Lexicographic ranking of the three official peers to the same personalized login greeting, including whether Sandbox's shared wiped environment is an acceptable first-success boundary.

uncertainties.question: Does the Sandbox route use the same Setup Wizard administrator fields and sample-app steps as Docker?
uncertainties.impact: If Sandbox omits or replaces Setup Wizard or sample-app steps, action and field counts diverge from the Docker reconstruction.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Complete current Sandbox guide walkthrough aligned to the same Express greeting terminal.
